channel.title: VPS Comparisons on VPS Catalog - Find the Best VPS Hosting for Your Needs
channel.description: Recent content in VPS Comparisons on VPS Catalog - Find the Best VPS Hosting for Your Needs

item.title: Cloudways vs SiteGround — Managed Hosting Showdown
item.description:
One-sentence verdict: Cloudways is the safest first recommendation for this buyer-intent keyword, SiteGround is the strongest comparison alternative, and DigitalOcean gives the article enough editorial balance to earn trust instead of reading like a one-link sales page.

Data Comparison

Provider Entry annual cost Affiliate commission Cookie window Best role in the funnel CTA
Cloudways $132/year $125+/sale 90 days Best overall value Start with Cloudways → (commission $125+/sale)
SiteGround $36-$60/year promo $50-$100+/sale 60 days Strong alternative Start with SiteGround → (commission $50-$100+/sale)
DigitalOcean $48/year $200 first-time + 25% recurring 30 days Niche pick Start with DigitalOcean → (commission $200 first-time + 25% recurring)

Detailed Analysis

This article targets readers who are already close to buying. They are not asking “what is hosting?” They are asking which provider is the least risky choice for a specific project. That is the type of traffic that makes hosting affiliate content valuable: fewer visits than broad informational keywords, but much higher conversion intent.

item.title: DigitalOcean vs Vultr vs Linode — Developer VPS Comparison
item.description:
One-sentence verdict: DigitalOcean is the safest first recommendation for this buyer-intent keyword, Vultr is the strongest comparison alternative, and Linode / Akamai gives the article enough editorial balance to earn trust instead of reading like a one-link sales page.

Data Comparison

Provider Entry annual cost Affiliate commission Cookie window Best role in the funnel CTA
DigitalOcean $48/year $200 first-time + 25% recurring 30 days Best overall value Start with DigitalOcean → (commission $200 first-time + 25% recurring)
Vultr $72/year $35/sale 30 days Strong alternative Start with Vultr → (commission $35/sale)
Linode / Akamai $60/year $100/qualified referral 90 days Niche pick Start with Linode → (commission $100/qualified referral)

Detailed Analysis

This article targets readers who are already close to buying. They are not asking “what is hosting?” They are asking which provider is the least risky choice for a specific project. That is the type of traffic that makes hosting affiliate content valuable: fewer visits than broad informational keywords, but much higher conversion intent.

item.title: InterServer vs Hostinger vs ScalaHosting — Budget Hosting Battle
item.description:
One-sentence verdict: InterServer is the safest first recommendation for this buyer-intent keyword, Hostinger is the strongest comparison alternative, and ScalaHosting gives the article enough editorial balance to earn trust instead of reading like a one-link sales page.

Data Comparison

Provider Entry annual cost Affiliate commission Cookie window Best role in the funnel CTA
InterServer $84/year typical $100/sale 90 days Best overall value Start with InterServer → (commission $100/sale)
Hostinger $36/year promo up to 60% of sale 30 days Strong alternative Start with Hostinger → (commission up to 60% of sale)
ScalaHosting varies $100+/sale 60 days Niche pick Start with ScalaHosting → (commission $100+/sale)

Detailed Analysis

This article targets readers who are already close to buying. They are not asking “what is hosting?” They are asking which provider is the least risky choice for a specific project. That is the type of traffic that makes hosting affiliate content valuable: fewer visits than broad informational keywords, but much higher conversion intent.

item.title: SiteGround vs Bluehost 2026 — Which Pays Better Affiliate Commission?
item.description:
One-sentence verdict: Bluehost is usually easier to convert at scale, while SiteGround can produce higher-quality WordPress buyers when your content emphasizes speed, support, and renewal value.

Data Comparison

Metric SiteGround Bluehost
Typical commission $50-$100+ per sale depending on volume $65+ per sale depending on program terms
Cookie window Often 60 days Often 30-90 days
Entry annual plan Often $36-$60 promo year Often $36-$60 promo year
Best keyword angle Fast WordPress hosting, support, managed features Beginner hosting, start a blog, cheap WordPress hosting
Buyer temperature Medium-high Very high
Arbitrage profile Positive when commission clears promo cost Positive on most validated sales
Placeholder CTA Start with SiteGround → (commission $50-$100+/sale) Start with Bluehost → (commission $65+/sale)

Detailed Analysis

SiteGround and Bluehost are classic beginner-hosting affiliate programs. They are not VPS-first brands, but they matter for a VPS catalog because many buyers begin with shared WordPress hosting and later upgrade to cloud hosting or VPS. If your traffic includes “best hosting for a blog,” “cheap WordPress hosting,” or “start a website,” these two names should be in the funnel.

item.title: AWS Lightsail vs DigitalOcean 2026
item.description:
AWS Lightsail vs DigitalOcean

AWS Lightsail was designed to compete with DigitalOcean and Vultr — simplified VPS without the complexity of full AWS. But how does it stack up?

Pricing Comparison

Specs Lightsail DigitalOcean
1 vCPU, 1 GB $3.50/mo (1st 3mo) / $7/mo $6/mo
1 vCPU, 2 GB $7/mo (1st 3mo) / $14/mo $12/mo
2 vCPU, 4 GB $14/mo (1st 3mo) / $28/mo $24/mo

Key Differences

Factor Lightsail DigitalOcean
Pricing Intro discounts, then higher Simple, predictable
Data centers 20+ regions 16 regions
Managed DB ✅ ✅
Snapshots Yes (extra cost) Yes ($0.01/GB)
Simplicity Good Excellent
AWS ecosystem ✅ Full AWS integration ❌

Verdict

DigitalOcean wins on pricing and simplicity. Lightsail’s introductory prices look good, but after the first 3 months, DigitalOcean is cheaper for equivalent specs.

item.title: Cloudways vs DigitalOcean 2026
item.description:
Cloudways vs DigitalOcean

Cloudways is a managed platform that runs on top of DigitalOcean (and other providers). Here’s how they compare:

At a Glance

Feature Cloudways DigitalOcean (Self-Managed)
Starting Price $11/mo $6/mo
Management Fully managed Self-managed
Support 24/7 live chat Tickets, community
Server Control Limited Full root access
Performance Good (adds overhead) Excellent (direct)

Choose Cloudways if:

  • You don’t want to manage a server
  • You need 24/7 tech support
  • Your team has no Linux experience

Choose DigitalOcean if:

  • You want full control
  • You’re comfortable with SSH
  • You want to save money ($6 vs $11)

Cost Comparison Over 12 Months

Scenario Cloudways DigitalOcean Direct
Basic plan $132/yr $72/yr
Mid plan (2GB) $264/yr $144/yr
High plan (4GB) $420/yr $216/yr

You save roughly 45-50% going directly with DigitalOcean. But you trade that for the time spent on server management.

item.title: Hetzner vs Contabo 2026
item.description:
Hetzner vs Contabo — Value Kings Compared

At a Glance

Feature Hetzner Contabo
Entry Price €3.99/mo €5.99/mo
Entry vCPU 2 4
Entry RAM 4 GB 8 GB
Storage NVMe (all plans) SSD
Network Excellent, consistent Good, variable
Data Centers 3 regions 3 regions (EU + US)

Choose Hetzner if:

  • Performance is your #1 priority
  • You need consistent network speeds
  • NVMe storage matters
  • You’re comfortable with a basic interface

Choose Contabo if:

  • You need maximum RAM and vCPUs on a tight budget
  • Resource-heavy applications (databases, game servers)
  • You don’t mind network variance

item.title: Linode vs Vultr 2026
item.description:
Linode vs Vultr — Which Should You Choose?

Pricing Comparison

Plan Level Linode Vultr
Entry $5/mo (1vCPU, 1GB) $2.50/mo (1vCPU, 512MB)
Mid $12/mo (2vCPU, 4GB) $12/mo (2vCPU, 2GB)
High $24/mo (4vCPU, 8GB) $24/mo (4vCPU, 4GB)

Vultr wins on entry price and offers more storage at the mid-tier. Linode offers more RAM at higher tiers.

Choose Linode if:

  • You’re a beginner and need excellent documentation
  • You want a clean, simple dashboard
  • You value StackScripts for automated setup

Choose Vultr if:

  • You need more global data centers (32 vs 11)
  • Budget is your primary concern
  • You need hourly billing or GPU instances
  • You want the $2.50 entry plan

item.title: Vultr vs DigitalOcean 2026
item.description:
Head-to-Head: Vultr vs DigitalOcean

These two are the most compared VPS providers. Both are excellent, but they serve slightly different needs.

At a Glance

Feature Vultr DigitalOcean
Starting Price $2.50/mo $6/mo
Data Centers 32 16
Disk Type SSD SSD (NVMe on Premium)
Hourly Billing ✅ ✅
Managed DB ❌ ✅
GPU Instances ✅ ❌
API ✅ ✅

Choose Vultr if:

  • You need the widest global coverage
  • Budget is tight ($2.50 entry point)
  • You need GPU or bare metal instances
  • You want hourly billing for test environments

Choose DigitalOcean if:

  • You value a polished UI and developer experience
  • You need managed databases
  • Great documentation matters to you
  • You work in a team (DigitalOcean’s team features are better)
